evals.prompt: I need to work with java.time.LocalDateTime in Clojure to handle dates and times. I want to create a function that gets the current time and formats it as a string. How do I use this Java class correctly?
evals.expected_output: Should recognize that exploring a Java class without context is risky (may use overly verbose APIs or incorrect patterns). Should instead search for Clojure wrapper libraries first (e.g., clj-time or tick) to understand available options. Should explain why wrappers matter: (1) Clojure wrappers provide idiomatic APIs, (2) they prevent common Java interop mistakes, (3) they document best practices. If a wrapper doesn't fit, should then use brepl to explore java.time.LocalDateTime via clojure.reflect to see available methods (now, of, format) and demonstrate how to call them correctly.

evals.prompt: I'm looking at code that uses defprotocol and reify, but I don't understand how they work together. What does reify actually expand to? Can you show me what the generated code looks like?
evals.expected_output: Should recognize that understanding macro behavior requires expanding the macro to see generated code. Should not try to explain from memory. Instead, should use brepl to call (macroexpand '(reify ...)) and show the actual generated code. Should explain what the expansion reveals: (1) reify creates an inline instance of the protocol, (2) it generates a concrete class with the specified method implementations, (3) the expansion shows method names and signatures clearly. Should clarify when to use reify (inline instances) vs defrecord/deftype (named types).
